reviewer_note: Abstract clearly rejects unaided clinician assessment accuracy and the agent shifted the majority from random yes to no.
No neuropsychological differences by LOC were reported, making this a clean no-collapse correction example.
The abstract directly states that FDG is not a good surrogate tracer for tumor hypoxia.
Routine station 9 lymph-node dissection is explicitly described as potentially unnecessary in selected lung cancer patients.
Continuing oral agents with bedtime NPH is clearly described as effective and well tolerated.
Severe hypoglycaemia is clearly associated with increased cardiovascular risk, while the agent predicted no in all seeds.
Aortic stiffness was not affected by acute heart-rate changes, yet both model families predicted yes.
The conclusion is genuinely mixed: overall OSA did not reduce fitness, but more severe OSA did.
